name: entry_base_collision
code:
proof fn phi_1_entry_base_collision(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < arch.num_entries(layer),
        j < arch.num_entries(layer),
        i != j,
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, j),
{
}
reason: Two distinct entry indices mapping to the same base address would cause virtual address aliasing in the page table

name: well_formed_allows_zero_entries
code:
proof fn phi_2_well_formed_allows_zero_entries(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.well_formed(),
    ensures
        d.entries.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: A well-formed directory with zero entries is degenerate and would mean the page table maps no address space at that subtree

name: inv_allows_subpage_entry_size
code:
proof fn phi_3_inv_allows_subpage_entry_size(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        arch.layers.len() > 0,
    ensures
        arch.entry_size((arch.layers.len() - 1) as nat) < 4096,
{
}
reason: A leaf-layer entry_size below 4096 bytes would violate the x86 minimum page size, making the architecture physically unrealizable

name: dir_base_vaddr_mismatch
code:
proof fn phi_4_dir_base_vaddr_mismatch(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Directory,
    ensures
        d.entries[i as int]->Directory_0.base_vaddr != d.base_vaddr + i * d.entry_size(),
{
}
reason: A child directory whose base_vaddr disagrees with its parent's layout would break the contiguous virtual address mapping invariant

name: entry_size_not_decomposable
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_size_not_decomposable(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(i) != arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat) * arch.num_entries((i + 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: If a layer's entry_size is not the product of the next layer's entry_size and num_entries, the hierarchical address space decomposition is inconsistent
